notes: Contract for future captured-building and supply-pile runtime state.
This file does not enable any production AP locations.
Production slot data must keep future location families empty until this contract is implemented in runtime and bridge code.

replayBehavior: Completed captured-building checks remain completed after mission restart or replay.
Duplicate capture events for an already completed runtime key are ignored.
Future pre-captured-building items may apply mission-start ownership, but the AP location itself is still shuffled separately and never grants itself.
enableBlockers: Runtime can identify the authored captured object without object-name guessing.
Runtime writes completed runtime keys through the same outbound bridge path as mission and cluster checks.
Runtime persists completed capture state before mission cleanup/restart can erase object state.
Bridge rejects unknown or unselected capture runtime keys instead of deriving IDs.

replayBehavior: Persistent collected amount and completed threshold keys survive mission restart or replay.
A threshold sends its AP location once when crossed, even if the mission is replayed.
Runtime must prevent repeat farming of already persisted pile progress before this family is enabled.
enableBlockers: Runtime can map an authored supply pile to collected amount without fragile object-name guessing.
Runtime persists pile depletion or equivalent collected amount across mission replay.
Runtime completes only selected threshold runtime keys from verified slot data.
Bridge rejects unknown or unselected supply runtime keys instead of deriving IDs.
